Modify the computeGenKill method to correctly handle array access nodes
In the computeGenKill method, add a check to see if the node is an array access node (e.g., a[1]). If it is, then recursively call the computeGenKill method on the base node (e.g., a) and the index node (e.g., 1). This will ensure that the variable is correctly identified as a name node.

Modify the addToSetIfLocal method to relax the precondition
In the addToSetIfLocal method, relax the precondition to allow non-name nodes to be added to the GEN or KILL set. This can be done by adding a check to see if the node is an array access node, and if so, then add the base node to the set instead.

Add a new method to handle array access nodes
Add a new method to the LiveVariablesAnalysis class that specifically handles array access nodes. This method can be called from the computeGenKill method when an array access node is encountered. The new method can recursively call the computeGenKill method on the base node and the index node, and then add the variable to the GEN or KILL set.
